channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Crowds demonstrate against gender-based violence in South Africa after alleged rape of 7-year-old
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa. (AP) — A crowd of protesters staged a demonstration against gender-based violence in the South African capital of Pretoria on Friday as part of a national outcry after an alleged rape of a 7-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A song critical of Nigeria's president is barred from airwaves
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — Authorities in Nigeria have barred radio stations from playing a song critical of President Bola Tinubu and his administration’s policies, according to a memo seen Friday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Walmart employee accused of killing co-worker inside store
item.description: A Walmart employee shot and killed a co-worker inside a Georgia store and critically wounded another worker outside early Friday while the business was closed to the public, authorities said. Newton County deputies responded to the shooting at the Walmart in Covington, Georgia, at around 1:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Global tariff battle escalates as China retaliates with 125% tariffs on US goods
item.description: China announced Friday that it will raise tariffs on U.S. goods from 84% to 125% — the latest salvo in an escalating trade war between the world’s two largest economies that has rattled markets and raised fears of a global slowdown. While U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NOW: 1 seriously injured in Salt Lake City rollover crash, road closed
item.description: One person is in critical condition after a rollover crash in Salt Lake City Friday morning, according to Salt Lake City Police. A road closure is in place at this time.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina prepares for second firing squad execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Long a method of execution associated with political terror or military justice, a firing squad is set to kill a South Carolina inmate on Friday, the second time the state will have carried out that method in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major nations agree on first-ever global fee on greenhouse gases with plan that targets shipping
item.description: Many of the world’s largest shipping nations decided on Friday to impose a minimum fee of $100 for every ton of greenhouse gases emitted by ships above certain thresholds, in what is effectively the first global tax on greenhouse gas emissions.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: All sense of survivors’ guilt was fleeting for those residents whose homes remained standing after wildfires ripped through the Los Angeles area three months ago.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER and SALLY HO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mayor: Family was to celebrate child's birthday when NYC sightseeing helicopter crashed, killing 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A family from Spain was about to celebrate the ninth birthday of one of their children when their sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey, killing all six people aboard in the latest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge scolds DOJ for demanding delay in mistakenly deported man case
item.description: The federal judge who ordered the Trump administration to try to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, scolded the Justice Department for demanding more time to update the court on next steps.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland immigrant wrongly deported to El Salvador must return to US, Supreme Court rules
item.description: The Supreme Court has ordered the Federal Government to facilitate the return of Salvadoran national Kilmar Abrego Garcia, of Maryland, back to the United States after he was unlawfully deported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USPS warns about common scams
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service and Postal Inspection Service want to warn consumers to be aware of fraud and spam regarding their mail, and what to do to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grand jury indicts man for threats against Augusta National employee
item.description: A Georgia grand jury has returned an indictment against a man for his role in making threats against an employee of the Augusta National Golf Club.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYPD searches for scooter-riding suspect who left brick with swastika on Tesla Cybertruck in Jewish community
item.description: The NYPD is searching for a scooter-riding woman who left trash and a brick with swastika on a Tesla Cybertruck in a Brooklyn Jewish community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mourners pay tribute to merengue icon Rubby Pérez, who died in the Dominican roof collapse
item.description: Fans and countrymen on Thursday shared their remembrances of Dominican Republic music icon Rubby Pérez, who was among the scores of people killed this week after the roof at the Jet Set club in Santo Domingo collapsed. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ moves to drop charges against man it labeled MS-13 leader
item.description: The move to dismiss the charges prompted the unusual push from Villatoro Santos’s lawyer seeking to delay the dropping of any charges against his client.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Giant bust of Elon Musk's head vandalized near Starbase in Texas
item.description: "We don't want this to happen again because it was an effort to put it up and very expensive," the owner said.
item.dc:creator: Mia Morales, NewsNation

item.title: Florida brings charges against a man jailed on federal counts of trying to assassinate Trump
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — A man already jailed on federal charges of attempting to assassinate then-presidential candidate Donald Trump as he golfed last fall will face additional state terrorism and attempted murder charges, Florida's attorney general said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: S&P 500 sinks 3.5% as Wall Street swings from euphoria back to worries about US-China trade war
item.description: The losses for U.S. stocks accelerated Thursday after the White House clarified that Chinese imports will be tariffed at 145%, not the 125% rate that Trump had written about in his posting on Truth Social Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Brawl erupts between college baseball teams
item.description: A fight broke out Tuesday night during a game between the Spring Hill College and University of Mobile baseball teams.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 19 Alabama restaurants claiming imported shrimp is locally sourced: report
item.description: "Until more enforcement is in place, Gulf Shores area residents and visitors are urged to stay vigilant," the release said.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department withdraws from plan to address discipline disparities for Native students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department has withdrawn from an agreement to address disparities in discipline for Native American students at a South Dakota school system, saying it was wrongly rooted in efforts to promote diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Trump tariff drama
item.description: Financial markets soared on Wednesday after President Trump issued a 90-day pause in almost all the tariffs that had roiled the global economy during the past week.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, NewsNation

item.title: Boozy battle: Maryland and Delaware fight over 'Orange Crush' cocktail
item.description: Lawmakers in Maryland voted Monday to approve the orange crush as the Old Line state's official state cocktail.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 96-year-old resident at southern Utah care home dies after being found in pool
item.description: A resident of a senior living facility in southern Utah has died after being found in the community's pool on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4
